articles.title: How Congo is battling an Ebola outbreak complicated by aid cuts, armed rebels, and anger
articles.description:
Experts say several crises combine to make the Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo difficult to contain.

The post How Congo is battling an Ebola outbreak complicated by aid cuts, armed rebels, and anger appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Experts highlight multiple crises complicating Ebola containment in eastern Congo.
Aid cuts and armed rebels hinder response efforts, affecting over 1 million residents.
Local Boston health officials monitor the situation due to potential global health risks.

articles.title: Bystander in serious condition after fatal shooting near White House checkpoint
articles.description:
A bystander who was struck by gunfire after a man fired on a checkpoint outside the White House remained in serious but stable condition Sunday.

The post Bystander in serious condition after fatal shooting near White House checkpoint appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: A bystander was shot during an incident near the White House checkpoint.
The victim remains in serious but stable condition as of Sunday.
Boston residents may feel heightened security concerns following this shooting.

articles.title: Pope Leo XIV makes historic apology for Vatican’s role in legitimizing slavery
articles.description:
No pope has ever publicly acknowledged, much less apologized for, the role that past popes played in giving European kings explicit authority to subjugate and enslave “infidels.”

The post Pope Leo XIV makes historic apology for Vatican’s role in legitimizing slavery appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Pope Leo XIV publicly apologized for the Vatican's historical role in legitimizing slavery.
This marks the first apology by a pope regarding slavery's endorsement by past popes.
Boston residents may see renewed discussions on racial justice and historical accountability.
